top_findings.summary: The skill requests Facebook Messenger login credentials (email and password) and proposes iMessage access for 2FA notifications. While Facebook credentials might be necessary for Messenger automation, requesting access to iMessage (a completely separate communication platform) for 2FA notifications represents permission overreach. A Messenger automation tool should handle 2FA through standard input methods or the same browser session, not by accessing unrelated messaging services. Additionally, the skill claims capabilities for "handling video and voice calls" which goes beyond typical messaging automation and may require excessive permissions.
